Title: Every record that Justin Bieber broke at Coachella in 2026
Sub-title: Here are all the records set and broken by Justin Bieber at Coachella 2026
Clean Content: Pursuits Every record that Justin Bieber broke at Coachella in 2026 Share : Next article Coachella 2026 or as fans have dubbed it, Bieberchella, hosted the comeback performance of Justin Bieber since he called it quits on his Justice World Tour in 2022 due to a major health crisis. His long-awaited comeback shows quickly took over everyone’s for you pages. With his raw, unfiltered, and stripped-down performance at Coachella 2026, Justin Bieber broke almost all the possible stereotypes and records the desert music festival has ever seen. Both Sabrina Carpenter and Bieber headlined the Indio desert music festival for two weekends with two strikingly different performances. Carpenter went full force with the theatrics, costumes, and set to give the viewers a complete glammed up performance, while Bieber showed up in a cozy hoodie and jorts paired with desert boots in an almost empty stage with one unrehearsed cameraman with a laptop and a water bottle. As you would expect, Bieber’s performance has invited a lot of criticism, with many calling it ‘lazy’ and ‘boring’. But, to hardcore fans, the sight of their idol happy, authentic, and singing with their own teenage selves, hit home hard. This year, Coachella welcomed over 125,000 attendees per day, and after the first weekend performances, the general entry tickets for the second weekend surged up to USD 3,000. The festival saw a record-breaking USD 200 million in revenue in one weekend. Justin Bieber was unmistakably the favourite headliner at the festival. Even though backlash and controversies never seem to leave his side, Bieber managed to make the most of this opportunity and broke most of the records the festival has ever seen. Here are all the records set and broken by Justin Bieber at Coachella 2026 Highest-paid artist at Coachella of all time For his two weekend performances in the desert, Bieber took home an incredible 10 million dollars. This salary accounted for his 90-minute-set per day, and constitutes the first nine-figure pay cheque in Coachella history, shattering the eight-figure payday record previously held by Beyoncé for her 2018 ‘Beychella’ performance. Merchandise and Skylrk sales Though Bieber’s nostalgic and evocative on-stage performance was the main attraction of the festival, the singer and his comparatively new fashion brand Skylrk saw amazing sales on both weekends. While Mrs. Hailey Bieber’s Rhode world was one of the main attractions for the beauty girls, Justin also had a 10,000-square-foot “SKYLRK Oasis” space installed in the heart of the Coachella festival grounds, with a Skylrk Shop right next to it. And without even actively inviting influencers, Skylrk raked in USD 5.04 million worth of product sales in the first weekend alone via email, closing the two weekend sales at a staggering USD 15 million and breaking the previous USD 1.7 million two-weekend sales record. Most-searched and most-viewed performance in Coachella history Even after 17 years, Bieber fever is alive and well. At Coachella 2026, Justin Bieber set a new record as the most viewed performer in Coachella history. His two weekend shows attracted millions of viewers from around the world, with his performance hitting 64.9 million views in just 12 hours. Bieber’s set also became the most googled and most-streamed performance ever in Coachella history. Highest ticket demand Justin Bieber was a key driver for the record-breaking demand for tickets that the music festival saw this year. His announcement broke all the previous records for ticket demand, with waitlists surging as well. And the demand turned to real sales, which brought the ticket sales revenue for a single weekend to USD 200 million. Most-viewed and most-liked post on Coachella’s Instagram After the first weekend’s performances came to an end, the post of Bieber closing with fireworks received an exceptional 168 million views and 13 million likes on Instagram, making it the most viewed and most liked post on Coachella’s official Instagram page. Huge streaming surge on Spotify According to Luminate , Bieber’s catalog generated 160.2 million on-demand official US streams in the April 10 to 16 tracking week, for just on his solo songs, following his first weekend performance in the desert. That shows an astounding 172 percent increase from the previous week (April 3 to 9), when his catalogue received 58.8 million streams. No. 1 global song on Spotify JB performed his 2012 hit “Beauty And A Beat” at the festival, hitting the Beliebers with a potent dose of nostalgia. After his set on Coachella weekend 1, the song climbed to No. 1 on Spotify Global Top 50 chart, dethroning BTS’ new single “Swim”. Billboard chart impact Bieber performed a set heavily focused on his latest albums SWAG and SWAG II , along with some of his older hits. He also brought out some iconic guest stars like SZA, The Kid Laroi, Billie Eilish, Big Sean, Dijon, and others over the two weekends. After Coachella, Bieber earned a new milestone when seven of his albums entered the Billboard 200 simultaneously. Four of his songs “YUKON”, “Daisies”, “Baby”, and “Beauty And A Beat” re-entered the Billboard Hot 100 list. But globally, he made an even bigger impact, with 17 songs re-charting on the Billboard Global 200. (Main and featured image: Kevin Mazur/Getty Images) Note: The information in this article is accurate as of the date of publication.
